title: The Book of General Ignorance
excerpt: A witty, eye-opening collection of common misconceptions and surprising facts that reveal how much we don't know about the world, from the creators of QI.
keyIdeas: Much of what we think we know is wrong — common knowledge is full of misconceptions
Questioning assumptions is essential for genuine understanding
Learning about our ignorance is more valuable than collecting facts
keyTakeaways: Many 'well-known facts' are actually incorrect or misleading
The most confident statements are often the most questionable
Understanding why we believe false things is as important as learning true things
History is full of misconceptions perpetuated by repetition
Science constantly revises our understanding of the world
whoShouldRead: Trivia enthusiasts who enjoy surprising facts
Fans of QI and panel show comedy
Anyone who enjoys having their assumptions challenged
Readers who want an entertaining introduction to general knowledge
whoShouldSkip: Those who prefer serious, academic treatment of facts
People who dislike the 'actually...' style of trivia correction
